items.title: Hungarian Mayor Tells Battery Maker to Leave Over Pollution Case
items.description: The mayor of Hungary’s second-largest city called on Chinese battery-parts maker Semcorp to walk away from a new, 65 billion-forint ($210 million) factory that’s been closed for alleged environmental

items.title: Slovakia to Revive Aluminum Plant That Was Once a Top Producer
items.description: Slovakia is set to revive primary aluminum production at a plant that was once one of Europe’s biggest producers of the metal.

items.title: AOL Owner Bending Spoons Jumps 14% After $1.68 Billion IPO
items.description: Bending Spoons SpA shares rose 14% above their initial public offering price after the company and some of its backers raised $1.68 billion.

items.title: Trump’s $1.4 Billion Haul Makes Him Biggest US Crypto Moneymaker
items.description: Donald Trump, who reversed his early skepticism of crypto to become one of the industry’s biggest boosters, reported more crypto-related income last year than any publicly traded US digital-asset comp

items.description: The segment discusses the current market sentiment that appears to be overestimating the likelihood of Federal Reserve interest rate hikes. Reference is made to the '12th blue dot,' a hypothetical add

items.title: Brightline Hurtles Toward Debt Deadline With No Plan in Place
items.description: It’s a date that lenders to Brightline, the debt-laden private Florida railroad, have had circled for months: July 1. That’s when the company will likely look to scrounge up what’s left in reserves to

items.title: UAE Oil Flows Hit Pre-War Levels on Dark Ships and Hormuz Bypass
items.description: The United United Arab Emirates has restored its oil exports to pre-war levels by sneaking tankers through the Strait of Hormuz undetected and using a pipeline that bypasses the waterway.

items.title: BlackRock Says Past Defaults, Next Election Cap Argentina Rally

items.title: Electric Bike Rental Firm Lime Rises 8% After $174 Million IPO
items.description: The operator of Lime electric scooters and bikes gained 8% after the company and some of its shareholders raised $174 million in an initial public offering.

items.title: This Heatwave Needs to Be Taken Seriously
items.description: The heatwave hitting the US right now needs to be taken seriously. Bloomberg Opinion columnist Mark Gongloff has advice on staying cool and how to detect heat exhaustion. (Source: Bloomberg)

items.description: While we wait for the jobs data.

items.title: New York, Chicago Grids Strain as Record Heat Wave Expands
items.description:
Power grids from Chicago to New York City are bracing for sky-high demand as sweltering heat begins to build across the eastern half of the US.

items.title: Stocks Bounce on Manufacturing, Warsh’s Remarks
items.description:
Signs of economic strength alongside easing price pressures spurred a bounce in stocks from session lows, with Federal Reserve Chair Kevin Warsh saying inflation risks have come down.
Jordan Jackson,

items.title: Warsh Taps Ex-BOE Chief King for New Fed Communications Task Force
items.description: Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh has tapped former Bank of England Governor Mervyn King to co-chair the US central bank’s new task force on communications.

items.title: US Aluminum Smelter Idled Since 2024 to Restart as Trump Looks to Revive Industry
items.description: An idled aluminum smelter in Missouri will restart production before the end of this year, helping to boost output of the metal amid the Trump administration’s push to revive the domestic industry.

items.title: Peru Inflation Unexpectedly Picks Up With Rising Food Prices
items.description: Inflation in Peru’s capital unexpectedly accelerated in June, staying above the central bank’s target range for a fourth straight month as rising food costs add to the recent global energy shock.

items.title: US Stocks Rise as Traders Weigh Warsh Remarks, Manufacturing
items.description: US stocks pared earlier losses as fresh economic data painted a picture of resilience, while traders assessed commentary from Federal Reserve Chairman Kevin Warsh.
